content: Hey, I'm so excited to be performing in Paris next month! I've been practicing my scales and I feel like my voice is stronger than ever.
That's great to hear! Paris is a beautiful city. What kind of technology do you use to help you prepare for your performances?
I use a digital recorder to practice and a tablet with a music notation app to study my scores. I'm also thinking of investing in a portable keyboard to make practicing easier on the road.
A portable keyboard is a great idea. Have you considered any specific brands or models?
Not yet, I've been doing some research but I'd love to hear some recommendations. By the way, I've been enjoying my free time gardening and playing chess with my friends.
Gardening and chess are great hobbies. I can suggest a few keyboard brands that are popular among musicians. What's your budget looking like for this purchase?
I'm thinking of spending around $1,000 to $1,500. I've also been trying to learn more about calligraphy in my free time, it's really interesting.
That's a good budget for a high-quality keyboard. Calligraphy is a beautiful art form. Are you taking any classes or teaching yourself?
I'm teaching myself through online tutorials and practice. It's been a fun challenge. My golden retriever, Willow, has been keeping me company while I practice.
That's great that you have a furry companion to keep you company. How's Willow adjusting to your busy schedule?
She's doing well, I've been making sure to spend quality time with her and take her on long walks. I'm actually planning a mushroom foraging trip next weekend and I'm thinking of bringing her along.
Mushroom foraging sounds like a fun adventure. Just make sure to keep an eye on Willow and ensure her safety. What kind of mushrooms are you hoping to find?
I'm not sure yet, I'll have to do some more research before the trip. I'm also thinking of cooking some Thai food for my friends when I get back, I love the flavors and spices.
Thai food is delicious. If you need any recipe recommendations or cooking tips, feel free to ask. Are you planning on performing any Thai-inspired songs in your upcoming show?
Not specifically, but I do have a few pieces that incorporate elements of international music. I'm really excited to share them with the audience in Paris on October 15th.
I'm sure it will be a great show. If you need any help with your tech setup or have any other questions, don't hesitate to reach out.
Thanks for the offer, I'll definitely keep that in mind. I should get going, I have a crossword puzzle to finish and then I'm meeting some friends for dinner at a new restaurant in the city.
Sounds like you have a fun evening ahead. Enjoy your dinner and crossword puzzle, and don't forget to take care of your voice before the big show!
